Identification.LongTitle: An Act to establish National Perinatal Bereavement Awareness Day
Identification.RunningHead: An Act to establish National Perinatal Bereavement Awareness Day

Provision.Text: This enactment designates October 15 in each and every year as âNational Perinatal Bereavement Awareness Dayâ.
Whereas each year in Canada, thousands of families are affected by perinatal bereavement;

Whereas perinatal death, considered one of the most stressful events that can occur in life, has a significant impact on the parentsâ psychological and physical health and can even lead to the breakup of their relationship;
Whereas those close to the bereaved parents often do not know what to say or do and feel powerless in the face of the parentsâ distress, which can contribute to the latterâs isolation;
Whereas it is important that the Parliament of Canada make the Canadian public aware of perinatal bereavement in order to help those affected by it;
Now, therefore, Her Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:

Section.Text: This Act may be cited as the National Perinatal Bereavement Awareness Day Act.
Throughout Canada, in each and every year, October 15 is to be known as âNational Perinatal Bereavement Awareness Day.â
For greater certainty, National Perinatal Bereavement Awareness Day is not a legal holiday or a non-juridical day.
